name: Prosperous Unification
description: Through its strife and triumphs, this society has reached every young civilization's ambition: a homeworld with unified goals, and a path open to the stars.
tooltip: - Initial Pops: +400 - Homeworld:  - ['concept_generator_district', Generator Districts]: +1  - ['concept_farming_district', Agriculture Districts]: +1  - Planetary Modifiers:  - Guaranteed Research Options: ['concept_planetary_unification', Planetary Unification]

name: Shroud-Forged
description: The guidance of a Shroud entity comes with a cost. Independent automata have started to operate freely among this networked society. Cut off from the Machine Intelligence, the demands of these units are ever-increasing.
tooltip: - Homeworld:  - : +4  - Start with 1000  - Start with Situation: Forged by the Shroud - Additional Starting Technologies:  - Unique Building:  - Available Planetary Decision:

name: Mindwardens
description: WARNING: Challenging Origin WARNING: Blocks access to .  This civilization's ancestors were plagued by otherworldly forces that invaded the mind, inspiring them to dedicate great effort and resources into developing anti-psionic technology.  Their ancient devices are still functional, but remain untested against psionic threats from the wider galaxy.
tooltip: - Additional Starting Technologies:  - Home System: Start with a Starhold. - Homeworld:  - Gain access to the Psionic Containment Casus Belli against psionically ascended Empires. - Guaranteed Habitable Worlds do not appear. - Mindwarden Enclaves are not generated at the start of game.

name: Endbringers
description: WARNING: Challenging Origin WARNING: Blocks all Ascension Paths except for .  This civilization has been long-obsessed with a mysterious calamity which turned a neighboring planet into a desolate and shrouded world. Now, as they take to the stars, they can finally explore its secrets and find out if a similar fate awaits them too.
negative_tooltip: - Empire starts with the modifier - Cannot form or join a

name: Evolutionary Predators
description: WARNING: Blocks all Ascension Paths except for .  This species has always hunted the most unique creatures, driven by an instinct they didn't fully understand. Possessing a rare ability to integrate the genetics of their prey, it evolved into the apex predator of its world, absorbing every genetic advantage available. Now, their hunger leads them to the stars.
tooltip: - Species Traits:  - Situation:  - Homeworld:

name: Starlit Citadel
description: A mysterious portal looms at the edge of this civilization's home system, a gateway from which hostile entities emerge in relentless waves. These invasions are as unpredictable as they are inevitable, forcing the people to rely on their greatest technological triumph - the Deep Space Citadel.
tooltip: - Home System:   - at the edge of the gravity well   - ['concept_wormhole', Strange Portal] leading to an unknown system  - Homeworld:   -   - ['concept_bioship_remains', Biological Ship Remains]   - ['concept_invasion_site', Invasion Site]: +2  - Additional Starting Technologies: ['concept_deep_space_citadel_tech', Deep Space Citadel]

name: Wilderness
description: WARNING: Blocks all Ascension Paths except for .  This entity is nothing less than a sapient ecosphere: a multitude of diverse lifeforms which long since coalesced to form a gestalt consciousness on a planetary scale.
tooltip: - Species Trait:  - Replaces all standard , , and with bespoke ones - Replaces Colony Ship Colonization with  - Unlocks new Resource:  - Homeworld:  - Size: 18  - ['concept_scorched_plains', Scorched Plains]

name: Treasure Hunters
description: Instilled with a thirst for adventure, this civilization is ready to set forth on their greatest and most challenging expedition. Armed only with a mysterious map, they now race across the stars to secure a legendary pirate treasure.
tooltip: - Starts with an inactive ['r_celestial_chart_0_desc', Celestial Chart] - Every recruited leader has a bonus trait - Ruler Traits:  -   -   -

name: Primal Calling
description: The rustle of bladed grass. The roaring silence of the night. The will of the hunter, sharp and cold as starlight...  This civilization has a primal connection to the wild. Viewing themselves as stewards to the diverse (and often deadly) wildlife native to their world, they now turn their gaze skyward. Masters of animal handling and ranching, this relationship with fauna has developed according to their ethics. How this bond will evolve on a galactic scale remains to be seen.
tooltip: - Homeworld:  - Planetary Modifiers:  - Home System: Space Fauna in a neighboring system - Available Buildings: ['concept_wildlife_ranch', Wildlife Ranch] - Can choose a special regarding Space Fauna upon First Contact - Additional Starting Technologies: ['technology:tech_genome_mapping', Genome Mapping] - Guaranteed Research Options: ['technology:tech_alien_life_studies', Xenology] - Gain insight on ['technology:tech_gravity_wells', Gravity Snares] after First Contact with Space Fauna - Gain insight on ['technology:tech_alien_cloning', Artificial Breeding] after capturing Space Fauna - Gain insight on ['technology:tech_controlled_mutations', Controlled Mutations] after cloning Space Fauna

name: Storm Chasers
description: There is no challenge they will not face, no lead they'll fail to chase - and no storm they will ignore.  At its core, this is a civilization seeking thrills. To its benefit, and to its detriment.
tooltip: - Leader Traits:  - Planet Unity from Jobs per Storm Building: +$@storm_chasers_storm_building_unity_produces_mult|%0$  - Cosmic Storm Appearance Chance Within Borders: +100%  - Cosmic Storm Technology Research Option Chance: +175% - Access to the  - Additional Starting Technologies:  -

name: Cybernetic Creed
description: WARNING: Blocks all Ascension Paths except for .  This empire pursues a divine calling, the fusion of exalted body and holy cybernetics. Augmentation is worship.
tooltip: - Homeworld: Initial buildings replaced by  - factions formed shortly after game start - Spiritualist Trait:  - Guaranteed Research Options: ['concept_integrated_cybernetics', Integrated Cybernetics] - Jobs

name: Synthetic Fertility
description: WARNING: Blocks all Ascension Paths except for .  Once a thriving society, a novel genetic disease has left this species unable to reproduce. Running out of options and facing extinction, they place their hope in digital salvation.
tooltip: - Initial Pops: +900  - Species Traits:  - Homeworld:   - Size: 20   - ['concept_abandoned_cities', Abandoned Cities]: +2   - ['concept_identity_repository', Identity Repository]   - ['concept_abandoned_gene_clinic', Abandoned Gene Clinic]  - Situation:  - Additional Starting Technologies:  - Guaranteed Research Options: ['concept_robotic_workers', Artificial Workforce]

name: Arc Welders
description: Their planet is barren, but their home system is a bounty of riches. To exploit it, these machines built bigger than ever before. A mighty Arc Furnace now dominates their sky, but these master engineers are far from satisfied.  The galaxy awaits, and the possibilities are endless. All it takes is the ingenuity to seize them.
tooltip: - Homeworld: Initial ['concept_mining_district', Mining Districts] replaced with ['concept_generator_district', Generator Districts]  - Home System: constructed around a  - Additional Starting Technologies: ['concept_orbital_arc_furnace', Arc Furnace]  - Guaranteed Research Options: ['concept_mega_engineering', Mega-Engineering] upon constructing a second Arc Furnace

name: Riftworld
description: A massive rift in space and time looms near this civilization's homeworld. After studying the phenomenon for millennia, recent advances in starship technology will allow them to finally reach out and discover what secrets it might hold.
tooltip: - Homeworld:  - Colonized Planets in systems with [ 'concept_astral_rift',Astral Rifts]:  - Chance for Astral Rifts to appear in systems you control: +100%

name: Under One Rule
description: Amidst violence and hardship, this civilization was unified by a single leader. Consolidating power through the repression of weaker factions, they have now become a fierce advocate for science and technological advancement. In the aftermath, an uneasy peace has descended. But peace rarely lasts.
tooltip: - Species Traits:  - Ruler Traits:

name: Payback
description: WARNING: Challenging Origin This civilization has been visited by an alien empire who forcibly enlightened and enslaved them. They have repelled their invaders, but will they return?

description: WARNING: Challenging Origin  This civilization began as a mutiny aboard a slaver vessel, and is made up of a diverse group of pre-FTL species.

name: Fear of the Dark
description: A century ago, one of the planets in this empire's home system was destroyed by a freak asteroid. Some believed this was a malicious attack by an alien species. Others brushed off these concerns as mere paranoia. The tension between the two groups grew so great that a newly-terraformed planet was granted to the fearmongers.
tooltip: - Home System: ['concept_preftl_civilizations', Pre-FTL Civilization] created by a Separatist Faction, formerly part of your Empire, granting events and benefits

name: Overtuned
description: This civilization uses any and all means of organic enhancement, no matter the risk, no matter the cost. After all, it is better to burn out brightly than fade away into obscurity.
tooltip: - Species Traits: Access to ['concept_overtuned_traits', Overtuned] Traits - Overtuned Traits:  - Can be boosted by the Edict  - Can be added and removed through Genetic Modification - Additional Starting Technologies: ['concept_gene_tailoring', Gene Tailoring]

name: Knights of the Toxic God
description: Centuries ago, this species' homeworld was visited by a Toxic Entity. The ecological catastrophe that followed ruined half the world, but allowed an early unification of the survivors in their reverence for the Toxic God. An Order of Knights has been created to search for the Toxic God, and although many things have changed, the quest continues.
tooltip: - Homeworld:   -   - Unique ['concept_pc_habitat', Orbital Habitat]:   - ['concept_order_keep', Order's Keep]   - ['concept_orders_demesne', Order's Demesne]   - ['concept_politician', Politician] Jobs replaced by  - Situation:  - Policies: Knightly Duties  - Available Holdings: ['concept_knight_commandery', Order's Commanderies] on Subject colonies

name: Progenitor Hive
description: This hive has gained evolutionary advantage through semi-independent leaders. These "Offspring" greatly improve overall proficiency, though they require constant oversight.
tooltip: - ['concept_spawning_pools', Spawning Pools] replaced with ['concept_offspring_nests', Offspring Nest] - Available Holdings: ['concept_offspring_holding', Offspring Nest] - If Devouring Swarm Civic is NOT selected: Sectors can be released as Subjects - Assigned Leaders: Gain additional passive experience - Available Starbase Buildings: Offspring Outlook - Can construct Offspring Ships

name: Subterranean
description: This civilization may never know why their ancestors migrated below the surface. Was it necessary to ensure self-preservation, or did the promise of bountiful resources allure them? Whatever the case, they successfully adapted, leading to a more environmentally versatile community.
tooltip: - Species Traits:  - ['concept_mining_district', Mining Districts]: Uncapped

name: Subterranean Machines
description: This civilization has flawlessly integrated into subterranean existence, initially driven by either calculated self-preservation or a directive to locate of hidden, abundant resources in the depths. The resulting mechanical adaptations have unearthed a versatile and unwavering community of machines.
tooltip: - Species Traits:  - ['concept_mining_district', Mining Districts]: Uncapped

name: Slingshot to the Stars
description: Once, the skies above this civilization were illuminated by shooting stars; then came a long season of darkness. Taking to the heavens in search of answers, the first offworld explorers discovered a dormant (and long abandoned) Quantum Catapult Single Array.
tooltip: - Home System: Nearby ruined with the  - Effects: Increased to +50% Ship Fire Rate

name: Teachers of the Shroud
description: WARNING: Blocks all Ascension Paths except for .  Long before their first steps onto the galactic stage, this civilization has been supported and shaped by a Shroudwalker coven.
tooltip: - Species Traits:  - Available Starbase Buildings:  - Communications with the  - Guaranteed Research Options: ['concept_psionic_theory', Psionic Theory].

name: Imperial Fiefdom
description: Our first endeavor beyond our home planet was a painful one, suddenly and brutally subjected to the whims of a foreign galactic power. Now we look towards a future in which we no longer live in the shadow of another empire.
tooltip: - Subject of an  - Can select a ['concept_specialist_subject', Specialist Subject] type at the start of the game

name: Here Be Dragons
description: Since time immemorial, this civilization has shared its star system with a formidable dragon. The mysterious creature's behavior sways between distant benevolence and haughty indifference. How this relationship will evolve as the planet dwellers take to the stars remains to be seen.
tooltip: - Home System: Inhabited by a protective Sky Dragon, that will become wrathful if displeased

name: Ocean Paradise
description: Since its birth, this civilization has enjoyed peace in a generous environment with no natural enemies, enabling unhindered growth and opulence.
tooltip: - Homeworld:  - (size: 30)  - Planetary Modifiers:   - Planetary Features:  - : Replaced with ['concept_pc_frozen', Frozen Worlds]

name: Subaquatic Machines
description: Since its creation, this machine civilization has delved deep into the underwater realms of an abundant ocean untouched by natural adversaries.
tooltip: - Homeworld:  - (size: 30)  - Planetary Modifiers:   - Planetary Features:  - : Replaced with ['concept_pc_frozen', Frozen Worlds]

name: Necrophage
description: This civilization evolved as a parasite, forced to feed off other sapient life forms to sustain itself. As it arrives on the galactic stage, other species will either be brought into the fold, or become its food. For the necrophage, there can be no other way.
tooltip: - Homeworld:  - Species Traits:  - Prepatent Pops: 1.2K  - : Replaced with
negative_tooltip: - Only Necrophage Pops can be Rulers or recruited as Leaders

name: Remnants
description: This civilization once spanned the void, controlling much of the galaxy. They were eventually defeated and almost destroyed, but after a long period of destitution they are returning to the stars.
tooltip: - Homeworld:  - (Size: 22)  - ['concept_ruined_arcology', Ruined Arcology]: +6 [GetRemnantAncientRelicEffect]

name: Shattered Ring
description: This civilization inhabits a Ring World built by unknown forerunners. If it can be understood and fully repaired it will grant them great power.
tooltip: - Homeworld:  - : Replaced with Colonizable Ring World Segments that have Blockers decreasing Habitability  - Species Traits:

name: Void Dwellers
description: This civilization has made its home in space for as long as it has maintained records - life in a typical planetary environment is anathema to them. They live in several orbital habitats that were only recently united.
tooltip: - Homeworld:  - Species Traits:  - ['concept_void_dweller', Void Dweller]  - ['concept_habitat_preference', Habitat Preference] - Additional Starting Technologies: ['concept_habitat_1', Orbital Habitats] - Guaranteed Research Options: ['concept_habitat_2', Habitat Expansion] - : Resource-rich systems instead of Colonizable Planets  - Can construct advanced housing buildings on Habitats - Waives Technology requirements for Basic District Specialization on Habitats

name: Voidforged
description: At first, the creators of this machine civilization designed them for eternal space travel, fully optimizing them to function seamlessly in zero gravity environments. After enduring eons of isolation, these machines have recently restored unity among the orbital habitats scattered throughout their home system.
tooltip: - Homeworld:  - Species Traits:  - ['concept_void_dweller_2', Zero-G Optimized]  - ['concept_habitat_preference', Habitat Preference] - Additional Starting Technologies: ['concept_habitat_1', Orbital Habitats] - Guaranteed Research Options: ['concept_habitat_2', Habitat Expansion] - : Resource-rich systems instead of Colonizable Planets  - Can construct advanced housing buildings on Habitats - Waives Technology requirements for Basic District Specialization on Habitats

name: Scion
description: A Fallen Empire has meddled in the development of this civilization for thousands of years, guiding them onto a path of their choosing.
tooltip: - ['concept_scion', Scion] of a Overlord - Home System: Connected to Overlord's capital via a  - Fallen Empire present in the galaxy regardless of Galaxy settings - Chance of receiving powerful gifts from Overlord

name: On the Shoulders of Giants
description: This civilization has hidden boons in their solar system, placed there in a distant past by a mysterious benefactor.

name: Common Ground
description: This civilization established early contact with their immediate alien neighbors. Finding strength in their differences, they soon decided to face the future, and whatever it might bring, together.
tooltip: - Leader of a non-Hegemony  - : Replaced with Federation members - Additional Starting Technologies:

name: Hegemon
description: This civilization established early contact with their immediate alien neighbors. Gradually, over the span of a century, they masterminded the birth of an interstellar union in which they would have a dominant role.
tooltip: - Leader of a  - : Replaced with Federation members - Additional Starting Technologies:

name: Doomsday
description: WARNING: Challenging Origin This civilization's homeworld is highly unstable, and it is only a matter of time before it explodes. Their only hope is to seek refuge elsewhere before it is too late.
negative_tooltip: - Homeworld: Planetary Modifier  - : Are NOT spawned

name: Calamitous Birth
description: Not native to their 'homeworld', these Lithoids arrived when a meteorite slammed into the planet and killed off most of the native life.

name: Life-Seeded
description: This civilization's home is a paradise, possibly designed just for them.
tooltip: - Homeworld: (Size: 30) with Rare Planetary Features - Species Traits:

name: Post-Apocalyptic
description: Baptized by nuclear fire, this civilization has faced total annihilation - and survived. Devastated yet unbroken, they have rebuilt civilization from the ashes of their old world.
tooltip: - Homeworld:  - Species Traits:

name: Radioactive Rovers
description: In the aftermath of a devastating nuclear disaster, these machines emerged as the sole survivors of a once-flourishing civilization. By continuing to fulfill their designated purpose, they gradually rebuilt society from the uninhabitable ashes of their old world.
tooltip: - Homeworld:  - Species Traits:

name: Clone Army
description: A species of short-lived and infertile soldier clones exceptionally proficient in matters of war, left masterless and alone to question their engineered purpose and uncertain fate.

name: Resource Consolidation
description: This Machine Intelligence has long-since consolidated all resources in their home system into their capital world, covering it entirely with machinery.

name: Mechanist
description: This civilization has been preoccupied with the idea of metallic automatons since the early Steam Age. Although many said it could not be done, the first true robots left the assembly lines long before even rudimentary space flight was achieved.
tooltip: - ['concept_robots', Robot] Pops: 800  - Mechanical Trait:  - Homeworld: ['concept_robot_assembly_plant', Robot Assembly Plant] - Additional Starting Technologies:  - ['concept_powered_exoskeletons', Powered Exoskeletons]  - ['concept_robotic_workers', Artificial Workforce] - Guaranteed Research Options: ['concept_robomodding', Machine Template System]

name: Syncretic Evolution
description: A second species forms an integral part of this civilization. They are big, strong, and most of them have the intelligence of a particularly dim-witted child. Ancient wars have culled their species of their most aggressive tendencies, leaving them quite servile.
tooltip: - Organic Pops with Trait: 1.2K

name: Tree of Life
description: This Hive evolved in a symbiotic relationship with a vast Tree. The Tree grants them many benefits, but its loss would cripple them.

name: Fruitful Partnership
description: This verdant civilization has developed a unique synergy with spaceborne fauna. By offering their fruits to these creatures, they entice the seed dispersers to aid their colonization efforts.

name: Galactic Doorstep
description: This civilization has a dormant Gateway in their home system - potential technological benefit, or a looming menace?

name: Lost Colony
description: This civilization originated as a lost and forgotten colony, separated from its homeworld long ago. The struggling colonists endured many hardships before they could build up the necessary technological and industrial base that would allow for a return to space.
